purpose: Review source-attested, structurally valid syllables missing from the current materialized inventory. This file does not assign Yinyuan IDs, codes, or keys.

de4.decision_basis: Wanxiang contains repeated colloquial phrase instances such as 嘚瑟; admission is restricted to multi-character source records.

se5.decision_basis: Wanxiang contains repeated colloquial phrase instances such as 嘚瑟; admission is restricted to multi-character source records.

kuai2.decision_basis: The local merged Unihan material records U+351E 㔞 as kuài and Wanxiang supplies the source-attested kuái reading; the review preserves the attested form rather than guessing a replacement.

kuang5.decision_basis: Wanxiang uses a neutral second syllable in reduplicated phrase records such as 框框; the local phrase source retains kuàng kuàng, so this variant remains phrase-only.

lan5.decision_basis: Wanxiang uses neutral lan in phrase records such as 栅栏; local single-character and phrase evidence otherwise uses lán, so neutral lan is not admitted for single-character records.

qiao5.decision_basis: Wanxiang uses neutral qiao in phrase records such as 蹊跷; the local phrase source retains qī qiāo, so this variant remains phrase-only.

sa2.decision_basis: Wanxiang supplies repeated regional food-name records using sá; local Unihan-derived material retains other readings, so this is recorded as a source-attested regional variant, not inferred from them.
